qwen3-8b: 5e-4 reached val acc=1.000 (vs 0.940/0.985 at 5e-5/1e-4). Epochs=5 won the epoch ablation: e3 underfit (acc=0.895), while e5 and e7 both hit acc=1.000 but e5 had the better margin (3.47 vs 3.28).
qwen3-1p7b: 5e-4 was the only stable LR (lower LRs collapsed to acc=0.000 with NaN margins). At lr=5e-4 only e3 was stable; e5 and e7 also collapsed to acc=0.000.
qwen3-14b: All three LRs hit acc=1.000; 5e-4 won by margin (6.99 vs 6.32/6.39) and 7x lower log_loss. All three epoch counts hit acc=1.000; e7 won by monotone margin growth (7.74 → 10.17 → 13.04) and 23x lower log_loss vs e3.
llama-3p1-8b-instruct: 5e-4 won by margin (10.13 vs 8.27/8.71) and 32x lower log_loss vs 5e-5. Epochs=3 monotonically dominated higher counts: e3 m=17.26 log_loss=7.6e-7, e5 m=15.29 log_loss=1.9e-3, e7 m=4.07 log_loss=0.068.
gemma-3-12b-it: 5e-4 had the highest val acc (0.985) — Gemma is the only model that didn't reach 1.000 at the best LR. Epochs=7 had monotone accuracy improvement (0.980 → 0.985 → 0.995) and 2.2x log_loss improvement (0.070 → 0.031); margin dipped at e5 then recovered.
